etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") + 舟(舟) (zhōu, "boat") = foot on a boat -> 前 (qián) "forward"; additional 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") for "cut" phoneticization
etymology.interpretation: 歬(qián) was initially an ideogrammic compound combining 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") and 舟(舟) (zhōu, "boat") to symbolize the concept of moving forward, as in 前(qián, "forward"). It also serves as a phonetic component in derivatives like 剪(剪) (jiǎn, "to cut"). The addition of 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") further differentiates it to represent cutting actions.
